The correct answer is (B) to point out the need to read novels and short stories in different ways.

The passage suggests that the writer views writing novels and short stories as two distinct processes that complement each other, creating a complete landscape. The writer alternates between writing novels and short stories, finding that each type of writing engages different parts of the brain and requires different approaches. The passage also highlights the benefits of writing short stories, such as their ability to be created quickly and their potential for experimentation and learning from mistakes.

The reference to a forest and a garden in the passage is a metaphor used to contrast the more complex and time-consuming process of writing novels with the more straightforward and quicker process of writing short stories. The writer finds that writing short stories is like planting a garden, while writing novels is like planting a forest. This metaphor suggests that short stories are more manageable and allow the writer to focus on different aspects of their craft, while novels require a more sustained and comprehensive approach.

Therefore, option (B) is the best answer because it accurately reflects the writer's view that reading novels and short stories in different ways is necessary to fully appreciate their unique qualities and benefits.

The answer is (C) Changing this arrangement would be impractical.

The writer suggests that alternating between writing novels and short stories has been a consistent pattern in their writing career, and that it takes some time to get off one track and switch to the other. They find that writing short stories provides a necessary change of pace and allows them to create stories out of small ideas, while novels require a more significant mental and physical commitment. The writer also mentions that short stories don't take as long to finish as novels, and that they can learn from their mistakes in short stories and apply that knowledge to their novels.

There is no indication in the passage that the writer would find it difficult or impractical to continue alternating between writing novels and short stories. In fact, the writer seems to enjoy the contrast between the two forms of writing and finds them complementary, as they allow them to explore different aspects of their fictional world and creativity.

The correct answer is (B) His readers were pleasantly surprised by them.

The passage explains that the writer finds writing short stories a joy because they are less demanding than writing novels. The writer states, "Writing short stories is more like planting a garden. The two processes complement each other, creating a complete landscape that I treasure." This suggests that writing short stories is a less challenging and less demanding task than writing novels, which is why the writer finds it enjoyable.

The passage also mentions that writing short stories allows the writer to experiment and try new things without the same level of pressure to succeed as with novels. The writer states, "In my case, when I write novels I try very hard to learn from the successes and failures I experience in writing short stories." This suggests that the writer finds it easier to take risks and try new things in short stories, which can lead to greater creativity and experimentation.

Overall, the passage suggests that writing short stories is less demanding and less pressure-filled than writing novels, which is why the writer finds it a joy.

Based on the given passage, the best answer for the question "In the first paragraph, the author suggests that one of his aims in the book was to" is (C) try to understand why great people lived the way they did.

The author states that he wanted to "sketch entertaining, small-bore portraits of the artist as a creature of habit" and "provide examples of how a variety of brilliant and successful people have confronted many of the same challenges." This suggests that the author's aim was to understand the daily habits and routines of great people in order to gain insight into their creative processes and personalities.

The author also mentions that he wanted to show how grand creative visions translate to small daily increments and how one's working habits influence the work itself, and vice versa. Overall, the passage presents a nuanced and thoughtful argument about the importance of daily routines, and the author's use of language reflects their mastery of the English language at the C2 level of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages.

The passage suggests that technology can enhance or hinder critical thinking, depending on the type of technology and how it is used. While technology can provide access to vast amounts of information and enable faster processing of data, it can also lead to information overload and a decline in the quality of thinking. The author notes that the wealth of communications and information available through technology can easily overwhelm our reasoning abilities, making it more difficult to think critically.

The passage also highlights the irony that despite the availability of more information than ever before, people are not necessarily making better decisions or thinking more critically. The author notes that a greater focus on visual media and multi-tasking may contribute to a decline in critical thinking, as these forms of media do not engage the imagination in the same way as reading.

Overall, the passage suggests that technology can have both positive and negative effects on critical thinking, and that it is important to understand the subtleties of how technology affects thinking and analysis in order to make the most of its potential.

The answer is (C) It has led to an increased awareness of critical thinking. According to Patricia Greenfield, a professor of psychology and director of the Children’s Digital Media Center, Los Angeles, the decline of literary reading has possibly contributed to a decline in critical thinking. Greenfield believes that a greater focus on visual media comes at a price, as it may undercut deeper thought and analysis. However, she also notes that exposure to technology fundamentally changes the way people think, and that reading enhances thinking and engages the imagination in a way that visual media do not.

The author acknowledges that the relationship between technology and critical thinking is not straightforward and that it is influenced by various factors, including the type of technology used and the way it is used. The passage highlights the need for further research to fully understand the impact of technology on critical thinking and to develop strategies for improving it.

Here's a possible blog post based on the information provided:

"Hey there, fellow adventurers! 🌴🐘

I hope this blog post finds you well. I'm Ella, and I recently returned from an incredible two-week volunteer trip to Thailand. 🇹🇭🌊

Last year, my sister Josie and I had the opportunity to volunteer at an elephant sanctuary in northern Thailand. 🐘💚 It was an experience of a lifetime, and I'm so grateful to have had the chance to be a part of it. 🙏

Our main job at the sanctuary was to collect the elephants' food. 🌽🥑 Can you imagine feeding a hungry herd of elephants? 😱 It took most of the day, but it was worth it to see those magnificent creatures enjoying their meals. 😊

We also helped wash the elephants in the river using big brushes and soap. 🌊🧴 It was so much fun, even though I got wetter than the elephants did! 😂

But the part of the day I most looked forward to was the evening. 🌃🌲 By the light of the moon, we led the elephants on foot into the forest area where they spent the night. 🌲😌 It was magical, and I felt so lucky to be a part of it.

The volunteers and staff stayed in the same house, and Josie and I shared a bedroom. 🛏️👧 It was hot and humid, especially at night, but we expected that. 🌞😅 The meals were delicious – and didn't include meat, which neither of us is keen on. 🍴🥩 After dinner, all the volunteers and staff spent the evenings talking and playing games. 💬🎮

Looking back, I'm so grateful for the experience. 🙏🌟 It was an incredible adventure, and I learned so much about elephants and their habitat. 🐘🌳 I also made some amazing friends from all over the world. 🌎🤝

If you ever get the chance to volunteer at an elephant sanctuary, I highly recommend it! 🐘💕 It's an experience you'll never forget. 🌟🌱

Until next time, stay wild and keep exploring! 🌳🌄

Cheers,
Ella 🐘💕"
